comparisons.sharedLightWorld: soft clinic daylight with facade bounce and mild dust haze
decisionReasons: stable daylight key and fill are easy to preserve internally
Luke and Amira both have strong readable protection under the same day profile

riskNotes: low risk if wall-bounce and dust haze remain stable

routeOptions.lightingHandling: internal base relight plus optional AI environment polish
riskNotes: must not re-key characters differently from clinic daylight world

routeOptions.lightingHandling: reference the same daylight world in start/end frames
riskNotes: continuity loss is unjustified unless camera/action spikes unusually high

comparisons.sharedLightWorld: single warm horizon key with skyline rim and restrained bloom
decisionReasons: the dramatic read depends on a stable sunset direction more than on complex motion
Luke/Amira protection channels can preserve different facial priorities inside one sunset world

routeOptions.lightingHandling: full internal relight with rim continuity
riskNotes: sunset rim side must remain fixed across all beats

routeOptions.lightingHandling: internal character relight plus optional sky enhancement
riskNotes: hybrid pass must not oversaturate Amira scarf or flatten Luke shirt values

routeOptions.lightingHandling: sunset world referenced by frames and packet notes
riskNotes: fallback is only warranted for unusually complex camera choreography

comparisons.sharedLightWorld: cool moonlit street with sparse practical spill pools
decisionReasons: night mouth readability and practical pools add complexity beyond a default internal pilot
the same moonlit world can still anchor all routes

routeOptions.lightingHandling: internal moonlight plus selective practical accent channels
riskNotes: highest risk is losing mouth readability in low-value beats

routeOptions.lightingHandling: internal character relight plus assisted practical spill polish
riskNotes: best compromise for preserving continuity while handling difficult practicals

routeOptions.lightingHandling: moonlit world described through packet plus reference frames
riskNotes: acceptable only if movement, crowding, or camera motion exceed hybrid envelope

comparisons.sharedLightWorld: overhead fluorescent pool with cool room bounce and limited local warmth correction
decisionReasons: the flat clinical world is predictable and package-friendly
the main challenge is protection-channel tuning, not spectacle

routeOptions.lightingHandling: internal character relight plus light compositing polish
riskNotes: hybrid should not create a second light source

routeOptions.lightingHandling: fluorescent world referenced but fully regenerated
riskNotes: fallback is rarely justified because internal control is more valuable here

comparisons.sharedLightWorld: soft domestic courtyard warmth with wall bounce and optional evening practicals
decisionReasons: domestic warmth is stable and favors internal continuity
the main work is preserving different emotional facial priorities, not solving spectacle

riskNotes: practical rise must not erase shared courtyard light

routeOptions.lightingHandling: internal character relight plus soft background/practical enhancement
riskNotes: hybrid pass should remain subordinate to the domestic sunset world

routeOptions.lightingHandling: courtyard warmth reproduced from packet references
riskNotes: fallback should be reserved for unusually complex ensemble blocking or camera work
